subsection.heading: Appointment of Inspector General.—
subsection.content: There shall be an Inspector General for the Commissions appointed in accordance with section 403(a) of title 5. All of the Commissions shall be subject to a single Inspector General.

paragraph.heading: In general.—
paragraph.content: A Commission shall maintain accurate and complete records of all its transactions and activities.

paragraph.heading: Availability.—
paragraph.content: All records of a Commission shall be available for audit and examination by the Inspector General (including authorized representatives of the Inspector General).

heading.inline: Records of Recipients of Commission Assistance.—

paragraph.heading: In general.—
paragraph.content: A recipient of funds from a Commission under this subtitle shall maintain accurate and complete records of transactions and activities financed with the funds and report to the Commission on the transactions and activities.

paragraph.heading: Availability.—
paragraph.content: All records required under paragraph (1) shall be available for audit by the Commission and the Inspector General (including authorized representatives of the Commission and the Inspector General).

subsection.heading: Annual Audit.—
subsection.content: The Inspector General shall audit the activities, transactions, and records of each Commission on an annual basis.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Pub. L. 110–234 and Pub. L. 110–246 enacted identical sections. Pub. L. 110–234 was repealed by section 4(a) of Pub. L. 110–246.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2022—Subsec. (a). Pub. L. 117–286 substituted “section 403(a) of title 5.” for “section 3(a) of the Inspector General Act of 1978 (5 U.S.C. App.).”

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective on the first day of the first fiscal year beginning after June 18, 2008, see section 14217(d) of Pub. L. 110–246, set out as a note under section 15101 of this title.
